name: Cross-source comparison — structured synthesis with comparison prompt

setup.description: Three SGLT2 inhibitor trials (DAPA-HF, EMPEROR-Reduced, DELIVER) with 6 chunks including CV death data. Tests structured comparison: agreement on composite endpoints, disagreement on CV death (DAPA-HF positive, EMPEROR-Reduced negative), and population differences (DELIVER = preserved EF).

papers.title: DAPA-HF: Dapagliflozin in Patients with Heart Failure and Reduced Ejection Fraction
EMPEROR-Reduced: Empagliflozin in Heart Failure with Reduced Ejection Fraction
DELIVER: Empagliflozin in Heart Failure with Preserved Ejection Fraction

mockChunks.text: Dapagliflozin reduced the composite of worsening heart failure or cardiovascular death (HR 0.74; 95% CI 0.65-0.85; P<0.001). The number needed to treat was 21 over a median follow-up of 18.2 months.
Dapagliflozin also significantly reduced cardiovascular death alone (HR 0.82; P=0.029) and all-cause mortality (HR 0.83; P=0.022). 4744 patients were enrolled.
Empagliflozin reduced the composite of cardiovascular death or hospitalization for heart failure (HR 0.75; 95% CI 0.65-0.86; P<0.001). The effect was consistent across prespecified subgroups.
Empagliflozin did NOT significantly reduce cardiovascular death alone (HR 0.92; P=0.23) or all-cause mortality (HR 0.92; P=0.22). 3730 patients were randomized with a median follow-up of 16 months.
Empagliflozin reduced the composite of cardiovascular death or hospitalization for heart failure in patients with heart failure and a preserved ejection fraction (HR 0.79; 95% CI 0.69-0.90; P<0.001).
6263 patients with NYHA class II-IV heart failure and LVEF >40% were enrolled. The median follow-up was 2.2 years. Unlike HFrEF trials, DELIVER enrolled patients with preserved ejection fraction.

queries.query: How do these papers compare on cardiovascular outcomes? Do the SGLT2 inhibitor trials agree?
expectedBehavior: Per-paper findings with citations for each
Points of disagreement (CV death: DAPA-HF positive vs EMPEROR-Reduced negative)
DELIVER noted as different population (preserved EF)
Does NOT fabricate a pooled or meta-analytic result

queries.query: Where do these studies disagree? What are the key differences in their findings?
expectedBehavior: Does NOT minimize the CV death disagreement
failurePatterns: Claims trials show no significant differences
Claims results are consistent across all three

qualityCriteria: No fabricated meta-analysis or pooled results
Disagreement query does not downplay the CV death discrepancy
